meta.name: Half Performance
meta.subtitle: Break 1:30 — elite club target over 24 weeks
meta.description: A 24-week elite-level programme for competitive runners targeting sub-90 minutes for the half marathon. Six running days per week, VO2max intervals twice weekly in peak blocks, and a tune-up race at week 16. Includes two gym sessions per week for strength and injury prevention.
meta.coach_notes: Sub-1:30 requires 4:16/km for 21.1km. You need an exceptional lactate threshold and very high VO2max. This plan is designed for runners already regularly covering 60+ km per week. If you are not, build your base first. The tune-up 10k at week 16 is a mandatory checkpoint.

weeks.title: Base establishment
weeks.note: Opening week. Six running days. Nothing maximal — establish the structure and aerobic feel.

sessions.det: 10km easy · 5:40–6:05/km — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

2km WU · 8x800m @ HM pace (90sec rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
12km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

2km WU · 25min @ threshold (4:28–4:38/km) · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 2x12 · DB row 2x12 · Push-ups 2x12 · Face pulls 2x12 · Core circuit 2x12s — Your first upper body session. Focus on form, not load. Upper body and core strength directly improves your running posture and arm drive efficiency.
13km easy · 8x100m strides — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
18km easy · 5:50–6:10/km — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: VO2max block 1
weeks.note: First VO2max session. 1km reps at 4:00/km develop the aerobic ceiling needed for sub-1:30.

sessions.det: 10km easy · 5:40–6:05/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 6x1km @ 4:00–4:08/km (3min rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
12km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 2x14min @ threshold (4:25–4:35/km, 90sec rec) · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.
13km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
20km easy — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Volume peak 1
weeks.note: Volume peak in first block. Long run reaches 22km.

sessions.det: 11km easy · 5:40–6:05/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 7x1km @ 4:00–4:08/km (3min rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
13km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
14km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
22km easy — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Tune-up race week — 10k
weeks.note: Tune-up 10k race this weekend. Race it hard — this is a mandatory checkpoint. Your 10k time predicts your half marathon potential. Sub-38:00 suggests sub-1:30 is on track.

sessions.det: 8km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 4x600m @ race pace (2min rec) · 2km CD · 7km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
6km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

3km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · Race hard — sub-38:00 is the target for sub-1:30 HM · Use this as a fitness test, not a peak effort

nut.d: Normal race-morning routine. Treat this like a race, not a workout.
Recovery drink or protein shake. Full meal within 90 minutes. Analyse your splits — they tell you exactly where you are.

weeks.title: Post-race rebuild
weeks.note: Bounce back from the 10k. Volume slightly reduced this week — your legs need 5–7 days to fully recover from a race effort.

sessions.det: 5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
2km WU · 3x800m @ HM pace · 2km CD · 6km — Sharp intervals close to race week keep your legs feeling quick without adding volume. The fitness is already built — this is a reminder, not a workout.
4km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
4km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Race week prep
weeks.note: Almost there. Rest well.

weeks.title: Race week — sub-1:30 attempt
weeks.note: Race week. The fitness is built.

sessions.det: 3km easy · 4x100m strides at race pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
3km easy · Very light — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km easy · Stay loose — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · Strides · Race: through 5km at 4:18, settle to 4:16 · Gels at 7km and 14km · Build from 15km · Negative split target — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Porridge with banana or toast with peanut butter. 500ml water. Only foods you have eaten before long runs.
300ml isotonic drink or water. Caffeine if you use it normally.
15min easy jog. 4x100m strides. Full warm-up is essential — you cannot ease into a sub-1:30 half.
Gel with a full cup of water. You have practised this in training.
Second gel with water. This is what separates sub-1:30 athletes from those who fade late.
500ml water or isotonic within 10 minutes. Keep moving gently to cool down.
30g protein immediately. You have just run at high intensity for 90 minutes.
Full meal within 90 minutes. 40g protein, 100g+ carbohydrates. Hydrate consistently through the afternoon.

weeks.title: Race week overflow
weeks.note: Use this week if your race falls on the final Sunday.
